step_by_step_thinking: I agree that option B, the dorsal column tract, is correct for this scenario. This tract primarily carries information related to fine touch and proprioception from the lower body upwards. The symptoms described—difficulty standing still with eyes closed due to loss of balance and needing to lift toes high before placing them down—are consistent with impaired proprioceptive input via the dorsal columns. No other tracts listed would explain these exact findings as well as the dorsal column does given its role in transmitting position sense and movement coordination sensations necessary for normal gait and stance stability.
